summary: The authors propose ALL-IN, a method that enables graph foundation models to generalize across datasets with different input features by projecting node features into a shared random space and using covariance-based statistics to achieve invariance to feature permutations and orthogonal transformations.

blocks.content: # Bridging Input Feature Spaces Towards Graph Foundation Models

source_span.quote: # Bridging Input Feature Spaces Towards Graph Foundation Models

blocks.content: In this work, we propose ALL-IN, a simple and theoretically grounded method that enables transferability across datasets with different input features.

source_span.quote: In this work, we propose ALL-IN, a simple and theoretically grounded method that enables transferability across datasets with different input features.

blocks.content: Our approach projects node features into a shared random space and constructs representations via covariance-based statistics, thus eliminating dependence on the original feature space.

source_span.quote: Our approach projects node features into a shared random space and constructs representations via covariance-based statistics, thus eliminating dependence on the original feature space.

blocks.content: We show that the computed node-covariance operators and the resulting node representations are invariant in distribution to permutations of the input features.

source_span.quote: We show that the computed node-covariance operators and the resulting node representations are invariant in distribution to permutations of the input features.

blocks.content: We further demonstrate that the expected operator exhibits invariance to general orthogonal transformations of the input features.

source_span.quote: We further demonstrate that the expected operator exhibits invariance to general orthogonal transformations of the input features.

blocks.content: Empirically, ALL-IN achieves strong performance across diverse node- and graph-level tasks on unseen datasets with new input features, without requiring architecture changes or retraining.

source_span.quote: Empirically, ALL-IN achieves strong performance across diverse node- and graph-level tasks on unseen datasets with new input features, without requiring architecture changes or retraining.

blocks.content: Unlike vision and language domains, graph learning lacks a shared input space, as input features differ across graph datasets not only in semantics, but also in value ranges and dimensionality.

source_span.quote: Unlike vision and language domains, graph learning lacks a shared input space, as input features differ across graph datasets not only in semantics, but also in value ranges and dimensionality.
